source.description: A reviewed six-panel pencil-and-colored-pencil process contact sheet.
source.notes: One consistent side-view wooden rocking horse on warm off-white paper. The curved rockers, body, legs, head, mane, tail, saddle, handles, wood tone, and shadow are established before the finishing panel.

process_strategy: The sequence fixes two long rocker arcs and one side-view horse gesture, builds the visible wooden body, neck, head, and supporting legs around a reserved saddle footprint, adds the mane, tail, ears, and muzzle, then establishes tack and restrained wood color before final cleanup only.

final_elements.name: paired rocker arcs, body oval, neck line, head block, and leg guides
final_elements.description: Light construction fixes two long curved rockers beneath an oval body, rising neck, small head wedge, and four angled leg guides.

final_elements.name: wooden body, neck, head, muzzle, and four legs attached to the rockers
final_elements.description: A clear side-view horse silhouette wraps the guides while four sturdy wooden legs connect the body to the established rocker rails.

final_elements.name: ears, eye, carved mane, tail, hoof ends, and rocker braces
final_elements.description: Two ears, one visible eye, a notched mane, simple tail, squared hoof ends, and two cross braces give the wooden toy its character and structure.

final_elements.name: saddle, bridle, handle peg, and carved wood seams
final_elements.description: A simple saddle covers the reserved back area while a bridle line, short handle peg, and a few restrained seam marks sit on established forms.

final_elements.name: warm brown wood, muted red saddle, ochre mane, graphite depth, and cast shadow
final_elements.description: Restrained colored pencil warms the existing wood, saddle, and mane while soft graphite adds depth and a grounding shadow.

frames.visible_job: Places two long rocker arcs, an oval body, rising neck line, head block, and four angled leg guides at one fixed scale.
introduces: paired rocker arcs, body oval, neck line, head block, and leg guides

frames.visible_job: Wraps the body, neck, head, muzzle, and four legs around the guides, connecting every leg to the established rocker rails.
introduces: wooden body, neck, head, muzzle, and four legs attached to the rockers
requires_prior_elements: paired rocker arcs, body oval, neck line, head block, and leg guides

frames.visible_job: Adds two ears, one eye, the notched mane, simple tail, hoof ends, and two structural braces to the existing toy.
introduces: ears, eye, carved mane, tail, hoof ends, and rocker braces
requires_prior_elements: wooden body, neck, head, muzzle, and four legs attached to the rockers

frames.visible_job: Adds a simple saddle over the reserved back area, then places the bridle, handle peg, and a few carved seams on established forms.
introduces: saddle, bridle, handle peg, and carved wood seams
requires_prior_elements: wooden body, neck, head, muzzle, and four legs attached to the rockers
ears, eye, carved mane, tail, hoof ends, and rocker braces

frames.visible_job: Adds warm brown, muted red, ochre, and graphite shading to the already established wood, saddle, mane, tack, rockers, and ground area.
introduces: warm brown wood, muted red saddle, ochre mane, graphite depth, and cast shadow
requires_prior_elements: wooden body, neck, head, muzzle, and four legs attached to the rockers
ears, eye, carved mane, tail, hoof ends, and rocker braces
saddle, bridle, handle peg, and carved wood seams

final_step.visible_job: Only strengthens established contours, joints, tack, wood seams, restrained colors, and the soft cast shadow.

rejection_checks: No rocker, body contour, leg, brace, ear, eye, mane, tail, saddle, bridle, handle, seam, color area, or shadow may first appear in the final panel.
The toy must retain one side view, stance, rocker curve, scale, crop, and leg arrangement across all six panels.
The saddle footprint must remain reserved; do not render finished wood grain beneath it and then cover it later.
Far legs must stay visually behind near legs using line breaks instead of crossing through the body.
Reject a live-horse pose, rider, nursery scenery, text, decorative border, glossy toy rendering, or vector clip-art finish.
